copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. Guardian PV Engine is a proprietary pharmacovigilance processing system.
title: Guardian PV Engine — Complete Pharmacovigilance Lifecycle
description: Full pharmacovigilance processing engine: ICSR validation (CIOMS I/II 4-element rule), causality assessment (Naranjo 10-question scale, WHO-UMC 6-category, RUCAM hepatotoxicity), seriousness classification (ICH E2A 7-criterion), expedited reporting determination (ICH E2D 7/15/90-day), PSUR/PBRER/DSUR interval calculation (ICH E2C/E2F), and case completeness scoring. Implements ICH E2A, E2B(R3), E2C(R2), E2D(R1), E2F, CIOMS I-VIII, and FDA 21 CFR 312.32/314.80. No external API calls — pure computation from regulatory reference data.

tools.name: assess-naranjo
tools.description: Run the Naranjo Adverse Drug Reaction Probability Scale. Scores 10 questions about temporal relationship, dechallenge, rechallenge, alternative causes, dose-response, and objective evidence. Returns total score (range -4 to +13) and category: Definite (>=9), Probable (5-8), Possible (1-4), Doubtful (<=0). Reference: Naranjo et al. (1981) Clin Pharmacol Ther 30:239-245.

parameters.name: q1_previous_reports
parameters.description: Previous conclusive reports on this reaction? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q2_temporal_relationship
parameters.description: Event appeared after suspected drug was given? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q3_dechallenge
parameters.description: ADR improved when drug was discontinued? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q4_rechallenge
parameters.description: ADR reappeared when drug was readministered? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q5_alternative_causes
parameters.description: Alternative causes could explain the reaction? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q6_placebo_response
parameters.description: Reaction appeared when placebo was given? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q7_drug_concentration
parameters.description: Drug detected in blood in toxic concentration? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q8_dose_response
parameters.description: Reaction more severe with increased dose? (yes/no/unknown)

parameters.name: q9_previous_experience
parameters.description: Patient had similar reaction to same or similar drugs? (yes/no/unknown)

tools.name: assess-who-umc
tools.description: Assess causality using the WHO-UMC system. Evaluates temporal relationship, dechallenge/rechallenge, and alternative causes to classify into 6 categories: Certain, Probable/Likely, Possible, Unlikely, Conditional/Unclassified, Unassessable/Unclassifiable. Reference: WHO-UMC Causality Assessment Guidelines.

parameters.name: time_relationship
parameters.description: Reasonable temporal relationship between drug and event?

parameters.name: dechallenge
parameters.description: Event improved when drug stopped (positive dechallenge)?

parameters.name: rechallenge
parameters.description: Event recurred when drug restarted (positive rechallenge)?

parameters.name: alternative_causes
parameters.description: Can alternative causes be ruled out?

parameters.name: known_response
parameters.description: Is this a known response pattern for the drug?

tools.name: classify-seriousness
tools.description: Classify adverse event seriousness using ICH E2A criteria. Checks 7 regulatory criteria: death, life-threatening, hospitalization/prolonged, disability/incapacity, congenital anomaly/birth defect, medically significant, requires intervention. CRITICAL: serious != severe. A severe headache is NOT serious. A mild heart attack IS serious (hospitalization). Reference: ICH E2A (1994), CIOMS IV (1998).

parameters.name: hospitalization
parameters.description: Required inpatient hospitalization or prolonged existing?

parameters.name: disability
parameters.description: Resulted in persistent or significant disability/incapacity?

tools.name: determine-expedited-reporting
tools.description: Determine expedited reporting type and deadline per ICH E2A/E2D and regional requirements. Evaluates seriousness, expectedness, fatality, DME status, and regulatory region to determine: 7-day (FDA IND fatal/life-threatening SUSAR), 15-day (serious unexpected), 90-day (serious expected), or non-expedited. Supports FDA, EMA, PMDA, Health Canada, TGA, MHRA, NMPA regions.

parameters.name: is_unexpected
parameters.description: Not in Reference Safety Information (Company Core Data Sheet)

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Regulatory region: fda, ema, pmda, hc, tga, mhra, nmpa, ich (default: ich)

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Report context: clinical_trial, post_marketing, literature, solicited (default: post_marketing)

parameters.name: is_study_endpoint
parameters.description: Event is a study endpoint (exempt from expedited reporting)

tools.name: calculate-submission-deadline
tools.description: Calculate regulatory submission deadline from Day 0 (awareness date). Per ICH E2D(R1), Day 0 = date minimum information received (4 elements: identifiable reporter, identifiable patient, suspect product, suspect reaction). Returns calendar and business day calculations with overdue status.

tools.name: calculate-psur-interval
tools.description: Calculate PSUR/PBRER reporting interval from International Birth Date (IBD). ICH E2C(R2) standard intervals: Years 1-2: every 6 months, Years 3-5: annually, Year 6+: every 3 years. Returns current period boundaries and 90-day submission deadline.

tools.name: calculate-dsur-cutoff
tools.description: Calculate DSUR (Development Safety Update Report) data lock point and submission deadline. ICH E2F: annual report during clinical development, data lock on DIBD anniversary, submission within 60 days.

tools.name: validate-icsr-minimum
tools.description: Validate ICSR against ICH E2B(R3) minimum data requirements. The 4-element rule: (1) identifiable reporter, (2) identifiable patient, (3) suspect product, (4) suspect reaction. Returns validity status, missing elements, and completeness score.

parameters.name: patient_age
parameters.description: Patient age or date of birth

tools.name: check-deadline-compliance
tools.description: Check if a case submission was within the regulatory deadline. Returns compliance status with days used, days over, and audit-ready details.

tools.name: list-capabilities
tools.description: List all Guardian PV Engine capabilities with their namespaces, parameter signatures, and regulatory references. Use this to discover what the engine can do.
